description: One realistic test prompt per skill package. These are smoke tests: they verify each skill has a usable trigger, workflow, output contract, and safety boundary.

evals.prompt: I had a messy operator day: reviewed a weekly report, answered five team questions, joined two meetings, researched an AI tool, wrote a handoff, and forgot to log two follow-ups. Turn this into a task log and tell me what could become skills.
evals.expected_output: A categorized task table with work type, automation potential, and best skill candidates.
expectations: The skill has required YAML frontmatter with name and description.
The skill defines Inputs, Workflow, Output, and Safety sections.
The expected output asks for a structured task log and skill candidates.

evals.prompt: Here is my task list from yesterday: meeting recap, report review, three email follow-ups, sales coaching prep, tool research, LinkedIn draft, team handoff, and updating a spreadsheet. Pick the 12 best AI skills from this.
evals.expected_output: A ranked list of skill candidates with job pain, input, output, safety boundary, and top 3 to build first.
expectations: The skill prioritizes repeated, structured, high-friction work.
The skill avoids automating final human judgment.
The output contract includes input, output, and safety boundary.

evals.prompt: Turn these meeting notes into a clear action plan: Maria owns the report review, James is checking partner follow-up, we agreed the training needs a pilot, and nobody picked a date for the dashboard fix.
evals.expected_output: Decisions, action items, owners, deadlines, open questions, risks, and optional follow-up message.
expectations: The skill tells the assistant not to invent owners.
The skill tells the assistant not to invent deadlines.
The output separates decisions, action items, and open questions.

evals.prompt: Draft a warm follow-up to a partner who asked for an update. I need to acknowledge the delay, say we are still working on it, and ask whether tomorrow morning is soon enough.
evals.expected_output: A recommended draft, optional softer version, subject line if email, and one-line reason the draft works.
expectations: The skill keeps follow-up output draft-only.
The skill prevents unapproved promises.
The output supports a softer version for sensitive topics.

evals.prompt: Explain in plain English how Arive updates flow into our database and then into GoHighLevel for follow-up. I need the filing-cabinet version and the risk points.
evals.expected_output: Plain-English workflow summary, system flow, risk points, next checks, and technical-owner questions if needed.
expectations: The skill uses Arive by name.
The skill uses GoHighLevel or GHL by name.
The skill avoids exposing passwords, secrets, or private configuration values.

evals.prompt: Explain this scorecard for a sales team: 12 people are above goal, 8 are close, 5 are behind, and we need to decide where coaching goes next.
evals.expected_output: Headline, three insights, what changed, what needs attention, recommended next action, and data gaps.
expectations: The skill tells the assistant not to invent causes behind numbers.
The skill translates metrics into action.
The output includes unknowns or data gaps.

evals.prompt: Prepare me for a coaching conversation with a sales professional who is below goal but improved activity over the last two weeks. I want to encourage the effort and set a clear next standard.
evals.expected_output: Conversation goal, affirmations, challenge, questions, next commitment, and optional opening script.
expectations: The skill keeps the tone direct and respectful.
The skill includes affirm and challenge sections.
The safety section prevents personal diagnosis.

evals.prompt: Build a practical playbook for following up with past customers after a market update. Audience is sales professionals. Keep it motivating and simple.
evals.expected_output: Title, goal, audience, when to use it, steps, examples, common mistakes, and checklist.
expectations: The skill turns a process into steps.
The skill includes examples and common mistakes.
The safety section flags unsupported or regulated claims.

evals.prompt: Review this public copy before we post it: We guarantee faster approvals than anyone else and will get every customer the best possible rate.
evals.expected_output: Risk flags, why each item is risky, safer wording options, review-needed items, and publish-readiness note.
expectations: The skill says it is not legal advice.
The skill flags guarantees and broad comparison claims.
The skill suggests safer wording instead of approving content.

evals.prompt: I tried an AI sales follow-up startup. It wrote fast drafts but had no approval step and did not understand regulated sales language. Turn this into a scorecard and founder message.
evals.expected_output: Product summary, useful parts, hype or weak spots, missing workflow pieces, would-pay-if condition, and founder message.
expectations: The skill separates useful, hype, missing, and worth-paying-for.
The skill drafts specific respectful founder feedback.
The safety section prevents claiming untested features.

evals.prompt: Write the LinkedIn post for launching this project: I tracked one workday, turned it into 12 AI skills, published the GitHub repo, and built the landing page.
evals.expected_output: A ready-to-post LinkedIn draft with hook, problem, action, lesson, and question.
expectations: The skill requires a specific closing question.
The style section avoids emojis, em dashes, filler, and unexplained jargon.
The safety section prevents exaggeration.

evals.prompt: I tried OpenClaw for two hours. Setup was confusing, but I can see how local agents could control files and workflows. Turn this into an honest field report for non-technical operators.
evals.expected_output: Field report with confusion, surprises, what worked, what failed, what feels powerful, what is too early, and recommendation.
expectations: The skill separates setup friction from product value.
The skill does not pretend early tools are production-ready.
The safety section warns against connecting sensitive systems casually.
